successCriteria.description: src/shared/types/index.ts exports EmploymentStatus enum with string values: Active, Inactive, Terminated, OnLeave
src/shared/types/index.ts exports LeaveStatus enum with string values: Pending, Approved, Rejected, Cancelled
src/shared/types/index.ts exports BaseEntity interface with fields: id (string), createdAt (Date), updatedAt (Date), deletedAt (Date | null)
src/shared/base.repository.ts exports abstract class BaseRepository<T extends BaseEntity> with protected abstract tableName: string and constructor accepting pg Pool
BaseRepository provides findById(id: string): Promise<T | null> — queries by id with deleted_at IS NULL filter
BaseRepository provides findAll(): Promise<T[]> — returns all non-soft-deleted rows
BaseRepository provides create(entity: Omit<T, keyof BaseEntity>): Promise<T> — generates UUID id, sets createdAt/updatedAt, inserts and returns the row
BaseRepository provides update(id: string, updates: Partial<Omit<T, keyof BaseEntity>>): Promise<T | null> — sets updated_at, updates only non-deleted rows, returns null if not found
BaseRepository provides softDelete(id: string): Promise<T | null> — sets deleted_at and updated_at to now, returns null if not found or already deleted

Jest unit tests exist in tests/unit/shared/ covering EmploymentStatus, LeaveStatus, BaseEntity interface conformance, and all five BaseRepository CRUD methods (findById, findAll, create, update, softDelete) with mocked pg Pool

constraints: BaseRepository must use the pg Pool from src/shared/db/connection.ts — no other database access mechanism
All database queries in BaseRepository must filter soft-deleted rows with deleted_at IS NULL (except softDelete itself)
BaseRepository.create must generate UUIDs via Node crypto.randomUUID()
Strict TypeScript: no implicit any, strict null checks — all method signatures must be fully typed
Tests must mock pg Pool.query — no real database connection in unit tests
outOfScope: Phase 2: Employee module — model and repository (employee.model.ts, employee.repository.ts, CreateEmployeeDto, UpdateEmployeeDto, index.ts)
Phase 3: LeaveType and LeavePolicy — models and repositories
Phase 6: LeaveRequest service — business logic and validation
Phase 7: AuditLog module — model, repository, and service
Phase 8: Notification module — model, repository, and service
Phase 9: LeaveRequest routes and controller with RBAC
Phase 10: LeaveBalance service — accrual and recalculation logic
Database migrations / Knex schema definitions for any tables — not in scope for this phase
Audit logging, RBAC enforcement, input validation at API boundaries — deferred to later phases
